name: Browse AI API
what_it_does: Browse AI — no-code web scraping and monitoring platform where robots are trained by recording browser interactions, with a REST API to trigger scrapes and retrieve structured data.
use_cases: Agents triggering Browse AI robots to extract data from websites that block traditional scrapers
Price monitoring — agents running Browse AI robots on competitor pricing pages and collecting changes via webhook
Job listing aggregation — agents using Browse AI to extract job postings from sites without public APIs
Social media data collection — agents triggering robots to extract profile stats and post metrics
Change detection — agents using Browse AI's monitoring mode to detect content changes on target pages
not_for: High-frequency bulk scraping at scale — Browse AI is optimized for periodic monitoring, not real-time bulk extraction
Developers preferring code-first scraping — use Apify or Playwright for programmatic control
Sites requiring deep JavaScript manipulation — Browse AI robots are limited by training recording capability
best_when: Your agent needs to scrape websites that block bots and you want to train extraction robots via browser recording without writing Playwright code.
avoid_when: You need programmatic, code-first web scraping at scale — use Apify or Playwright for developer-controlled extraction.

auth.notes: API key from Browse AI dashboard as Bearer token. Account-level access — no scope granularity. Robots are identified by ID in API calls.

pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing — each robot run consumes credits. More complex sites consume more credits. Monitoring runs consumed at reduced credit rate.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent robot runs: plan-dependent (default 5); API calls: 100 req/min

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Basic REST API. Error responses include status and message. Robot execution failures may return success HTTP status with error in response body — agents must inspect body.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Robot creation is idempotent with same config. Each robot run creates a new task — running same robot twice consumes double credits. Results are immutable once completed.
known_agent_gotchas: Robot execution is async — trigger returns task ID; agents must poll task status or use webhooks for completion
Credit consumption is unpredictable — complex pages or CAPTCHA encounters may consume extra credits
Robots must be pre-trained via UI — agents cannot create training robots purely via API
Target site changes can break robots — agents should handle extraction failures and retrain robots when needed
US-only infrastructure means GDPR/EU data compliance requires careful evaluation for European data sources

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. No SOC2 or compliance certifications documented. API key with no scope granularity. US-only data processing. Use with caution for GDPR-regulated data extraction.
